---
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from protest_impact.data.news.config import newspapers_of_record, popular_newspapers, local_newspapers, all_newspapers_with_id
from protest_impact.util import project_root
import pandas as pd
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm

count_data = []
for newspaper in tqdm(all_newspapers_with_id.keys()):
    path = project_root / "data" / "news" / "fulltext" / newspaper
    if not path.exists():
        continue
    for date in path.iterdir():
        if not date.name.startswith("20"):
            continue
        count = len(list(date.iterdir()))
        count_data.append(dict(date=date.name, newspaper=newspaper, count=count))
count_data = pd.DataFrame(count_data)
count_data["date"] = pd.to_datetime(count_data["date"])
count_data.head()
```

```{python}
from itertools import chain

df = count_data.copy()
# compute monthly averages for each newspaper
df = df.groupby([pd.Grouper(key="date", freq="W"), "newspaper"]).mean().reset_index()
# cap values at 50
df["count"] = df["count"].clip(upper=20)
# split into multiple dataframes for plotting
df_newspapers = df[df["newspaper"].isin(newspapers_of_record)]
df_popular = df[df["newspaper"].isin(popular_newspapers)]
df_local = df[df["newspaper"].isin(chain(*local_newspapers.values()))]
dfs = [df_newspapers, df_popular, df_local]
[len(df) for df in dfs]
```

```{python}
import altair as alt


def plot(df):
    # year labels in x axis
    return (
        alt.Chart(df, width=1200, height=100)
        .mark_line()
        .encode(
            x=alt.X("date:T", axis=alt.Axis(format="%Y-%m")),
            y="count:Q",
            row="newspaper:N",
            tooltip=["date:T", "count:Q", "newspaper:N"],
        )
    )
```

```{python}
plot(df_newspapers)
```

```{python}
plot(df_popular)
```

```{python}
plot(df_local)
```

```{python}
import numpy as np
df= count_data.copy()
# sample data down to 100 days per newspaper
# df = df.groupby("newspaper").apply(lambda x: x.sample(1000, replace=True))
# ungroup
# df = df.reset_index(drop=True)
# sample down to 8% of the data
df = df.sample(frac=0.08)
# make counts relative to the average daily count for each newspaper
df["count"] = df["count"] / df.groupby("newspaper")["count"].transform("mean")
# apply log transform
df["count"] = df["count"].apply(lambda x: np.log(x))
print(len(df))
# plot distribution of articles per day for each newspaper (with matplotlib)
alt.Chart(df, width=100, height=50).mark_bar().encode(
    x=alt.X("count:Q", bin=alt.Bin(maxbins=50)),
    y="count()",
    column="newspaper:N"
)
```

```{python}
# retrieve holidays for germany from 2013 to 2022
import holidays

german_holidays = holidays.Germany(years=range(2013, 2023))
```

```{python}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from datetime import datetime
for year in range(2005, 2023):
    df = count_data.copy()
    start = datetime(year, 1, 1)
    end = datetime(year, 12, 31)
    df = df[(df["date"] >= start) & (df["date"] <= end)]
    # make values relative to the average daily count for each newspaper
    # df["count"] = df["count"] / df.groupby("newspaper")["count"].transform("mean")
    # collect newspapers that have articles in the selected time range
    newspapers = df[df["date"] >= start]["newspaper"].unique()
    # plot the data for each newspaper (with matplotlib)
    # use the object oriented interface of matplotlib
    fig, axs = plt.subplots(len(newspapers), figsize=(20, 10), sharex=True)
    for ax, newspaper in zip(axs, newspapers):
        df[df["newspaper"] == newspaper].plot(x="date", y="count", ax=ax)
        ax.set_title(newspaper)
        # highlight weekends, holidays, and month boundaries
        for date in pd.date_range(start, end):
            if date.dayofweek >= 5:
                ax.axvspan(date, date + pd.Timedelta(days=1), color="orange", alpha=0.1)
            if date in german_holidays:
                ax.axvspan(date, date + pd.Timedelta(days=1), color="red", alpha=0.1)
            if date.day == 1:
                ax.axvspan(date, date + pd.Timedelta(days=1), color="black", alpha=0.2)
        ax.set_xlim(start, end)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    exit()
    # export as pdf
    fig.savefig(f"../../data/data_{year}.pdf")
    plt.close(fig)
```
